schema_note: Criticality tier is INDEPENDENT of volume. This is deliberate: a low-volume programme can be Tier 1 (nutritionally critical) while a high-volume programme is Tier 3. A planning report that sorts by quantity alone will systematically bury Tier 1 low-volume programmes.

programmes.name: Mid-Day Meal - Government Schools
programmes.nutritional_flag: Balanced calorie/protein meal, no special micronutrient mandate
programmes.reversibility_if_missed: High - can be made up next day without medical consequence

programmes.name: Anganwadi Take-Home Ration (Iron-Fortified)
programmes.nutritional_flag: Iron-fortified; targets anemia in children under 6; requires green leafy vegetables + fortified rice as recipe-locked inputs
programmes.reversibility_if_missed: Low - part of a medically supervised anemia intervention; a missed day breaks the supplementation cycle

programmes.name: Adolescent Girls Anemia & Nutrition Programme
programmes.nutritional_flag: Iron + folic acid fortified meal; clinically tracked cohort
programmes.reversibility_if_missed: Low - clinically tracked; missed doses are reported upstream to health department

programmes.name: Mid-Day Meal - Government Schools (New Expansion Order)
programmes.nutritional_flag: Standard meal, but politically/contractually high-visibility as a fresh government commitment
programmes.reversibility_if_missed: Medium - reputational and contractual exposure if a brand-new government order is missed on day one

programmes.name: Pregnant & Lactating Women Nutrition Support
programmes.nutritional_flag: Fortified khichdi/porridge; recipe shares fortified rice with PRG-002
programmes.reversibility_if_missed: Low - maternal nutrition programme, missed servings flagged to health authorities
